Q4 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. If a language L is finite, then the minimal DFA accepting L contains no cycle.
2. Every DFA accepting a finite language must contain a dead state.
Which of the above statements are true?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Q5 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : There exist no algorithm for deciding if any two Turing Machine M1 and M2 accept the same language.
S2 : The problem of determining whether a Turing Machine halts on any input is undecidable.
Which one of the following options is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
S1 : Equivalence test of two TM is undecidable.
S2 : Halting problem of TM is undecidable.

Q9 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements about regular languages are true?

Correct answer: A, B, C, D
Solution

(a, b, c, d)
• Option (a) regular languages are closed under intersection.
• Option (b) regular languages are closed under union.
• Option (c) regular languages are closed under complement.
• Option (d) regular languages are closed under Kleene operation.

Q10 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the regular expression R = (a + b)* (aa + bb) (a + b)*
Which deterministic finite automaton accepts the language represented by the regular expression R?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
DFA given in option (a).
Here, S3 and S4 are equivalent states and can be minimized.

Q11 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following problems are decidable?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
CFL’s are not closed under complementation and a program can loop forever. So, it may not produce any output.
Regular and recursive languages are closed under complementation.
Hence, only 3, 4 are decidable.

Q14 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Let <M> be the encoding of a Turing Machine M.
Which of the following languages are decidable?

Correct answer: A, B, C, D
Solution

(a)
(a) 2024 steps in finite. So it is decidable.
(b) Membership problem of a TM is undecidable.
(c) Membership problem of a TM is undecidable.
(d) It is non-trivial property. Hence undecidable.
